rulings.Case Title: RICHARD SHAW VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 29, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Rockwell Automation Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed February 2. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: EUGENE MILLARD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 29, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Exxon Mobil Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. As to the causation issue, Mr. Hagen¿s deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. As to duty, defendant¿s separate statement failed to properly make an issue of whether defendant owed a duty to plaintiff for affirmative acts of its employees. Even if defendant had properly made duty an issue, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that defendant owed no duty to plaintiff. SeaBright Ins. Co. v. US Airways, Inc. (2011) 52 Cal.4th 590 is inapplicable. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN R. LEONARD et al VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 29, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant John Crane, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ negligence and strict liability causes of action are denied. The deposition testimony of Mr. Leonard and Mr. Balcena create a triable issue whether Mr. Leonard worked with, or around others working with, John Crane packing. As to asbestos content of that packing, even if Mr. Springs¿ declaration is admissible, it merely creates a triable issue when read in conjunction with Mr. Balcena¿s deposition testimony. As to Mr. Leonard¿s exposure, Dr. Millette¿s deposition testimony in this case and Mr. Cohen¿s declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Leonard was exposed to John Crane, Inc. packing. The declaration of Dr. Bruch creates a triable issue whether such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Leonard¿s disease. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
